sanskrit:
रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत।
रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा।।
transliteration: rajas tamaś cābhibhūya sattvaṃ bhavati bhārata | rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamaś caiva tamaḥ sattvaṃ rajas tathā ||
literal_meaning: O descendant of Bharata, sometimes goodness prevails over passion and ignorance; sometimes passion overcomes goodness and ignorance; and at other times ignorance overcomes goodness and passion.
contextual_meaning: The mind is a battlefield where three competing motivational systems—clarity (sattva), craving (rajas), and confusion (tamas)—take turns dominating perception, mood, and action.
psychological_state: Rapidly shifting ego-states; unstable self-concept driven by whichever guna is momentarily ascendant.
trigger_scenario: You wake up motivated (sattva), scroll social media and feel envy (rajas), then binge-eat while doom-watching news (tamas) all before noon.
anchor_text: Notice which inner mood—clarity, craving, or confusion—is steering you right now.
